name: NYC Transit Live Subway Nearest
description: Find nearby NYC subway stations with live train arrivals near a WGS84 coordinate. Returns stations with distance, walk time, served lines, and per-train arrival predictions (line, Uptown/Downtown direction, minutes). Does not geocode - caller must resolve place names to lat/lng first.

usage.callShape: POST https://transit402.dev/subway/nearest with JSON body {lat, lng, limit?}. Pay via MPP on Tempo ($0.02). additionalProperties:false - send only lat/lng/limit.
usage.outputShape: results[].{name, distance_feet, walk_minutes, lines[], arrivals[].{line, direction(Uptown|Downtown), minutes}, lat, lng}
quirks: lat/lng required (WGS84 decimal degrees); limit optional integer 1-10, default 5. additionalProperties:false.

PAYMENT: plain x402 'exact' does NOT work and qa-pay cannot complete it - first 402 returns application/problem+json type https://paymentauth.org/problems/payment-required carrying a challengeId. Pay via MPP protocol on Tempo instead (agentcash fetch paymentProtocol:'mpp'); settled $0.02 with real txHash 0xd206b5b2e1d0b7294217d638db0671e310ef780caca0dfa498eae0b8612d8a7c.
Server advertises protocols [x402, mpp]; mpp is the reliable path.
No geocoding - resolve place names to lat/lng before calling.
arrivals direction enum is exactly Uptown|Downtown.
usage.guide: Find nearby NYC subway stations with live train arrivals near a WGS84 coordinate. Returns stations with distance, walk time, served lines, and per-train arrival predictions (line, Uptown/Downtown direction, minutes). Does not geocode - caller must resolve place names to lat/lng first. POST https://transit402.dev/subway/nearest with JSON body {lat, lng, limit?}. Pay via MPP on Tempo ($0.02). additionalProperties:false - send only lat/lng/limit. Output: results[].{name, distance_feet, walk_minutes, lines[], arrivals[].{line, direction(Uptown|Downtown), minutes}, lat, lng}

name: NYC Transit Live Bus Nearest
description: Find nearby NYC bus stops with live ETA predictions near a WGS84 coordinate. Returns stops with distance, walk time, served routes, and per-bus arrival predictions (route, destination headsign, minutes, proximity text, stops_away). Does not geocode - caller must resolve place names to lat/lng first.

usage.callShape: POST https://transit402.dev/bus/nearest with JSON body {lat, lng, limit?}. Pay via MPP on Tempo ($0.02). additionalProperties:false - send only lat/lng/limit.
usage.outputShape: results[].{name, distance_feet, walk_minutes, routes[], arrivals[].{route, destination, minutes(nullable), proximity(nullable), stops_away(nullable)}, lat, lng}
quirks: lat/lng required (WGS84 decimal degrees); limit optional integer 1-10, default 5. additionalProperties:false.
Output differs from subway: uses routes[] (not lines[]) and arrivals carry route/destination/proximity/stops_away.
arrivals.minutes, proximity, and stops_away can each be null (bus not yet predicted with an ETA, only a distance/proximity string).

PAYMENT: plain x402 'exact' fails and qa-pay can't complete the paymentauth.org challengeId handshake (402 problem+json). Pay via MPP on Tempo (agentcash fetch paymentProtocol:'mpp'); settled $0.02 with real txHash 0x43eb51b4a0f422a65399eac3cfe4fe9950688adc004cae1534a03f32ca8e84bf.
No geocoding - resolve place names to lat/lng before calling.
usage.guide: Find nearby NYC bus stops with live ETA predictions near a WGS84 coordinate. Returns stops with distance, walk time, served routes, and per-bus arrival predictions (route, destination headsign, minutes, proximity text, stops_away). Does not geocode - caller must resolve place names to lat/lng first. POST https://transit402.dev/bus/nearest with JSON body {lat, lng, limit?}. Pay via MPP on Tempo ($0.02). additionalProperties:false - send only lat/lng/limit. Output: results[].{name, distance_feet, walk_minutes, routes[], arrivals[].{route, destination, minutes(nullable), proximity(nullable), stops_away(nullable)}, lat, lng}

name: Japan Transit Station Search
description: Fuzzy-search Japanese train stations by name (accepts kanji, kana, or romaji; tolerates the 駅/station suffix). Returns matching stations with canonical station_id, EN/JA names, operator_ids, line_ids, and lat/lng. Use to resolve a station name to a canonical record before calling status.

usage.callShape: GET https://transit.agentic-jp.com/stations/search?q=Shinjuku  (query param `q` required, min 2 chars; NOT `query`/`search`)
usage.outputShape: query (string, echoes input), count (number), results[] each: station_id, name_ja, name_en, name_kana, operator_ids[], line_ids[], lat, lng
quirks: Required query param is `q` (NOT `query` or `search`); min 2 chars; accepts kanji (新宿), kana, or romaji (Shinjuku) and tolerates the 駅/station suffix.
Registry inputSchema is null but the real schema is discoverable from the x402 402-challenge bazaar extension.
name_kana is frequently null (e.g. Shinjuku); lat/lng can be null for some stations (e.g. Seibu-Shinjuku).
station_id is operator-prefixed (e.g. toei-oedo-shinjuku) — the same physical station can appear multiple times under different operators.
Price $0.001 = atomic amount 1000 USDC; x402 rails available on Base/Polygon/Solana.
usage.guide: Fuzzy-search Japanese train stations by name (accepts kanji, kana, or romaji; tolerates the 駅/station suffix). Returns matching stations with canonical station_id, EN/JA names, operator_ids, line_ids, and lat/lng. Use to resolve a station name to a canonical record before calling status. GET https://transit.agentic-jp.com/stations/search?q=Shinjuku  (query param `q` required, min 2 chars; NOT `query`/`search`) Output: query (string, echoes input), count (number), results[] each: station_id, name_ja, name_en, name_kana, operator_ids[], line_ids[], lat, lng

name: Japan Transit Station Status
description: Real-time delay and operational status for a Japanese train station, sourced live from ODPT. Returns per-line status (normal/delayed/suspended), estimated delay minutes, cause (JA/EN), staleness flag, plus one-line EN and JA summaries. Use to answer 'is my train delayed' for a station.

usage.callShape: GET https://transit.agentic-jp.com/station/{name}/status  — station name is a PATH param (kanji/kana/romaji), NOT a query param; no body
usage.outputShape: station{name_ja,name_en,operator,lines[](JA line names)}, status{overall, lines[]{line_id,line_name_ja,line_name_en,status,delay_minutes_estimated,reason,reason_en,updated_at,stale}}, summary_en, summary_ja, generated_at
quirks: Station name is a URL PATH param ({name}), NOT a query param.

status.overall and per-line `status` use enum normal/delayed/suspended; a line can be flagged `delayed` even when reason text says no >15min delay, so treat `status` as the canonical signal and check `delay_minutes_estimated` (often null).
reason/reason_en are often null or Japanese-only; English context is in summary_en.
Each line carries its own `stale` flag and `updated_at` (mixed timezones: ODPT lines use +09:00, fallbacks use Z) — check `stale` before trusting.
Best used after stations-search to get a resolvable name; provider resolves romaji/kanji directly.

Price $0.005 = atomic amount 5000 USDC; x402 rails on Base/Polygon/Solana. Note: live discover_api_endpoints quoted the status price as $0.01 but the actual paid call settled at $0.005 (matches registry).
usage.guide: Real-time delay and operational status for a Japanese train station, sourced live from ODPT. Returns per-line status (normal/delayed/suspended), estimated delay minutes, cause (JA/EN), staleness flag, plus one-line EN and JA summaries. Use to answer 'is my train delayed' for a station. GET https://transit.agentic-jp.com/station/{name}/status  — station name is a PATH param (kanji/kana/romaji), NOT a query param; no body Output: station{name_ja,name_en,operator,lines[](JA line names)}, status{overall, lines[]{line_id,line_name_ja,line_name_en,status,delay_minutes_estimated,reason,reason_en,updated_at,stale}}, summary_en, summary_ja, generated_at

name: Waze Traffic
description: Waze traffic alerts and live traffic-jam data for a location. Pay-per-call x402.

usage.callShape: GET https://stableninja.dev/api/waze/alerts-and-jams?center=LAT,LON&radius=5&radius_units=KM&max_alerts=5&max_jams=5 (all params optional; specify a location via center+radius+radius_units, or via bottom_left+top_right bounding box)
usage.outputShape: body.status ("OK"), body.data.alerts[] (each: type, subtype, description, street, city, latitude, longitude, num_thumbs_up, publish_datetime_utc), body.data.jams[] (each: level, speed_kmh, length_meters, street, end_location, line_coordinates[], update_datetime_utc), body.parameters (echo of resolved query incl computed bottom_left/top_right)
quirks: All query params are optional but you must specify a location somehow; center=LAT,LON requires radius and radius_units (KM|MI) alongside it. Coordinates are LAT,LON comma-separated strings matching pattern ^-?\d+(\.\d+)?,-?\d+(\.\d+)?$. Cap max_alerts/max_jams (0-100) to keep the response small.
usage.guide: Send a GET to https://stableninja.dev/api/waze/alerts-and-jams with a location in the query string. Simplest: center=LAT,LON plus radius (e.g. 5) and radius_units=KM (or MI); alternatively pass a bounding box via bottom_left and top_right. Optionally cap results with max_alerts and max_jams (0-100). Pays $0.01 USDC via x402 on Base. Read body.data.alerts[] for incidents/hazards/closures and body.data.jams[] for live congestion (level, speed_kmh, length_meters, street). Plain synchronous JSON, no auth beyond payment.

name: Directions + Travel Times (keyronne)
description: Point-to-point directions between 2-10 stops by car/bike/foot: total + per-leg distance and travel time, plus route geometry (encoded polyline). Stops can be place names (geocoded) or {lat,lon}. Global, OpenStreetMap data. Fills the routing/ETA gap.

quirks: stops: 2-10 items; each a place-name string (auto-geocoded) OR a {lat,lon} object.
mode enum: car | bike | foot (default car).
Returns an encoded polyline, NOT a rendered map image (see gaps.md for static-map rendering).
usage.guide: POST a JSON body {stops:[...], mode} to sequence an itinerary and check a day is feasible. Total and per-leg distanceKm/durationMinutes come back plus a geometry polyline. Pay ~$0.01 USDC on Base via x402.

name: Relaystation Route (OSRM mirror)
description: Route between two points — distance, duration, geometry as JSON. Redundant fallback for keyronne.

quirks: travelMode enum: Car|Truck|Pedestrian|Scooter.
Also accepts EURC on Base.
usage.guide: Fallback point-to-point route if keyronne is unavailable. ~$0.01 USDC on Base.
